title: Suggestion
description: Abstract base type for nodes that indicate a suggested change to content.
$comment:
This is an abstract base type used in Stencila Schema for suggested document changes.

It exists to represent proposed edits as first-class nodes that can carry
status, feedback, provenance, and original or replacement content. This
supports review and model-assisted editing workflows that need suggestions to
persist within the document model.

Key properties include the suggestion `suggestionType`, `status`, and any
associated feedback or authorship metadata.

analogues.name: word processor tracked change
analogues.notes: Close editing analogue for persisted proposed changes, though Stencila suggestions are explicit typed nodes rather than markup overlays in a word-processing format.

type.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@type` keyword.

id.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@id` keyword.

suggestionType.description: The type of suggestion including whether it is an insertion or a deletion.

suggestionStatus.description: The status of the suggestion including whether it is the original, or is accepted, or rejected.

provenance.description: A summary of the provenance of the content within the suggestion.

datePublished.description: Date of the suggestion was created.
datePublished.$comment:
Provides consistency with `Comment` (and other `CreativeWork` node types)
as the property for storing the date of creation of the suggestion.

executionDuration.description: Time taken to generate the suggestion.

executionEnded.description: The timestamp when the generation ended.
